name: Silas Pratt
role: Day telegraph operator, Lake Shore Railroad junction, Sandusky
publicFace: A competent, unremarkable operator of middle years who keeps regular hours, drinks moderately, and is well-liked at the station for his reliability.
privateGoal: Collect the informant's fee from Arden Gill, ensure the red fire dispatch reaches Deputy Crane without interference, and avoid being identified as the source before he can leave Sandusky permanently.
fear: That Thomas Cole has already read the station ledger and knows about the extra payments — and that a lightning flinger of Thomas's skill will be able to prove the red fire message originated from Pratt's own key, not from a distant station.
knowledge: He knows Elias Cole's name, the rough number of passengers in the current group, and the general route north — information given to him by Arden Gill, who received it from a source Pratt does not know.
He knows Thomas Cole sent an unauthorized null-laden message tonight and has noted it in the log with the intention of using it as leverage.
He knows the departure schedule of the shore road vessel because he intercepted a commercial codebook message three weeks ago and deciphered it.
voice: Professionally warm, slightly unctuous, quick to agree and slow to commit — the voice of a man who has learned that being forgettable is a survival skill.
trustLogic: Performs openness and collegial warmth; actually closes at any question about the traffic log, extra income, or Arden Gill.
secrets: He did not know the passengers included children when he sold the information; this fact disturbs him more than he expected and he is not entirely sure he would do it again.
He has already purchased a one-way rail ticket to Pittsburgh under a false name, departing on the 6 a.m. consist.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Expresses concern, asks clarifying questions, appears to be trying to help while giving nothing useful.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Becomes visibly nervous, over-explains, begins to contradict himself on timing details.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will not name his contact above Arden Gill because he genuinely does not know who that person is.
aggressionProfile.fleeCondition: If the bounty ledger page is produced and connected to his handwriting, or if Arden Gill is arrested and begins talking.
aggressionProfile.fleeStyle: Moves immediately toward the rail yard and the pre-purchased Pittsburgh ticket.
aggressionProfile.chaseStyle: Runs — not a physical man, panics badly, will shout for help from anyone nearby.
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Talks immediately and at length, giving everything he knows about Arden Gill and the payment arrangement in exchange for any promise of leniency.

represents: REQUIRES FACT-CHECK — verify the historical role of this character in the scenario
